**What I verified (all clean):**
- Descriptor-only posture is honest: `production_ready=false`, `runtime_ready=false`; no production gate weakened.
- Coverage matches baseline/plan/contract: 40 units (12/22/6), distribution 23/5/2/6/2/2, range RP17.P01.M02.S09–M04.S06.
- No-write/no-real-data boundaries pervasively attested; source is pure frozen-object generation; test only reads.
- Hermes is evidence-only (`emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false`, not embedded in product runtime).
- Claude stays read-only and non-final-approver; human approval required; auth preflight passed with valid JSON.
- Source↔contract↔plan consistent (validator deep-equals projections; historical binding tail = CP00-515).

**One P3 (cosmetic, non-blocking):** `freezeCp515Result` in `packages/ai-governance/src/service.js:196` carries RP16-style dispatch labels (`dispatches_dlp_runtime`/`retention`/`legal_hold`) instead of RP17 AI-governance labels — mirrored into the contract at lines 3012-3015. All flags are `false` and the correct AI-governance no-dispatch attestation is present in the enforced nested `no_write_attestation`, so the boundary is intact. Recommend renaming in a follow-up to match `freezeCp514Result`.

No P0/P1/P2 issues. The CP514 counterpart freezeCp514Result (lines 27-37) correctly uses dispatches_ai_governance_runtime/dispatches_model_policy_runtime/dispatches_citation_runtime/dispatches_retrieval_scope_runtime. Because the RP17 validator deep-equals the contract against this result, the same mislabeled fields are mirrored into contracts/ai-governance-core-contract.json p01_implementation_slice_descriptor (lines 3012-3015). Impact is cosmetic/descriptor-hygiene only: every flag is false (safe direction), and the semantically-correct AI-governance no-dispatch attestation (dispatches_ai_governance_runtime/model_policy/citation/retrieval_scope = false) IS present in the nested no_write_attestation and enforced by the contract, manifest implementation_summary, and model.test.js.
